{"problem_standardized_math":"Verify that the multipliers \\(\\lambda_i\\), \\(i\\in\\mathcal I\\), defined by\n\\[\n\\lambda_i=\n\\begin{cases}\n0, & \\text{if } -c_i(x)+\\lambda_i^k/\\mu_k\\le 0,\\\\\n\\lambda_i^k-\\mu_k c_i(x), & \\text{otherwise},\n\\end{cases}\n\\tag{17.63}\n\\]\nindeed attain the maximum in\n\\[\n\\hat F(x;\\lambda^k,\\mu_k)\n=\\max_{\\lambda\\succeq 0}\n\\left\\{\n f(x)-\\sum_{i\\in\\mathcal I}\\lambda_i c_i(x)\n-\\frac{1}{2\\mu_k}\\sum_{i\\in\\mathcal I}(\\lambda_i-\\lambda_i^k)^2\n\\right\\},\n\\tag{17.62}\n\\]\nand verify that\n\\[\n\\hat F(x;\\lambda^k,\\mu_k)\n= f(x)+\\sum_{i\\in\\mathcal I}\\psi(c_i(x),\\lambda_i^k;\\mu_k).\n\\tag{17.64}\n\\]\nHint: Use the KKT conditions for the problem\n\\[\n\\max\\ \\phi(x)\\qquad \\text{subject to } x\\ge 0,\n\\]\nwhich imply that at a stationary point, either \\(x_i=0\\) and \\([\\nabla\\phi(x)]_i\\le 0\\), or \\(x_i>0\\) and \\([\\nabla\\phi(x)]_i=0\\)."}
